stemHtml:
A pilot uses a Lambert conformal chart for navigation. At a waypoint (A), the course of the route is changed by the FMS in order to follow the next route segment, which is indicated as a straight line on the chart. What is the initial great circle track angle (M) after the passage of waypoint A?

Standard parallels of the Lambert chart: 45°N and 61°N.
The segment: from A (45°N 120°W) to B (45°N 100°W)
Magnetic variation: 12°W
